_note: Round-8 follow-up #5: when two inline typed dispatchers declare the SAME event name with incompatible detail types, upstream's `addToEvents` (ComponentEvents.ts:279) detects the collision and pushes the name into `dispatchedEvents`, which `toDefString` emits last as `'name': customEvent` — the JS object-literal override semantic collapses both typed spreads to `CustomEvent<any>`. (See upstream's `ts-event-dispatchers-same-event/expectedv2.ts:23`.) Pre-fix native intersected the typed sources at the type level — `({hi: boolean}) & ({hi: string})` made `hi`'s detail `boolean & string` = `never`, which TS rejected on every consumer handler. Post-fix native walks all inline type-literal members across all typed dispatchers via `collect_inline_typed_dispatcher_member_names`, finds names appearing 2+ times, and pushes each duplicate into the untyped-names layer; round-7 #5's `Omit<typed, keyof untyped> & untyped` layer order then drops the duplicate from the typed mapped type and overrides with `CustomEvent<any>`. Inner here declares two `<{ hi: ... }>` dispatchers; Consumer's `on:hi={handleAny}` (handler typed `CustomEvent`) passes — pre-fix this fired TS2345 because the type collapsed to `CustomEvent<never>`.
